title: Understanding the MACD Indicator
content: Moving Average Convergence Divergence (MACD) is a trend-following momentum indicator that shows the relationship between two moving averages of a security's price. The MACD is calculated by subtracting the 26-period Exponential Moving Average (EMA) from the 12-period EMA. A nine-day EMA of the MACD called the 'signal line', is then plotted on top of the MACD line, which can function as a trigger for buy and sell signals.

title: What is the P/E Ratio?
content: The price-to-earnings ratio (P/E ratio) is the ratio for valuing a company that measures its current share price relative to its earnings per share (EPS). The P/E ratio is calculated by dividing the Current Stock Price by the Earnings Per Share (EPS). A high P/E could mean that a company's stock is overvalued, or else that investors are expecting high growth rates in the future.

title: Return on Equity (ROE)
content: Return on equity (ROE) is a measure of financial performance calculated by dividing net income by shareholders' equity. Because shareholders' equity is equal to a company's assets minus its debt, ROE is considered the return on net assets. ROE is considered a measure of a corporation's profitability in relation to stockholders' equity.
